compiled_state.title: T5: schema-coverage guard test + per-command output-contract docs
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description: Coverage-guard test enumerating every structured output, asserting each validates against a schema AND no structured output lacks a schema. Document the per-command output contract + schema source-of-truth decision + drift guard. Link schemas from README/CLAUDE/docs.

comments.body:
TARGETS (derived; to be confirmed). Story = schema-coverage guard test + per-command output-contract docs. Depends on T1-T4 (all schemas + --output commands exist).

NEW TEST:
- tests/interfaces/test_schema_coverage.py — enumerate EVERY structured (--output json) output across CLI/library/MCP; assert (a) each validates against a registered schema AND (b) no structured output lacks a schema (coverage enforced, not aspirational). Drive via a registry mapping command->schema name.

REGISTRY:
- src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py — a COMMANDS/coverage map (command/interface -> schema) as the single source the guard test consumes.

DOCS:
- README.md — document the canonical --output/-o flag + per-command output contract; link schemas.
- CLAUDE.md (tool guide) — MCP tool set + outputSchema note; flag change.
- docs/event-schema.md — link the output schemas (note: schemas are replay-derived state, distinct from events).
- docs/architecture.md — interface/output-contract section.
- Decision doc: schema source-of-truth (hand-authored JSON Schema files vs pydantic-generated) + drift guard if both coexist (the MCP pydantic models vs schema files — cross-interface test pins both).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
TARGETS — VERIFICATION CORRECTIONS (Explore audit). On-track; specify before building:
* COMMAND_SCHEMA_MAP shape: dict command-name -> schema-name, interface-agnostic (reused for CLI/lib/MCP via adapters). Lives in schemas/__init__.py as the single source the guard consumes.
* COVERAGE assertion: guard reads the map, drives each command via the adapter pattern (rebar_repo fixture + Library/Cli/Mcp adapters from test_schema_ticket_state.py), validates output vs mapped schema, AND cross-checks the dispatcher subcommand list (parse _print_overview / case arms) so any structured-output command MISSING from the map fails the test (coverage enforced).
* "structured output" == --output json (post-T2 unified flag). Reword AC away from --json/--format.
* SOURCE-OF-TRUTH decision to document: hand-authored JSON Schema files are the source of truth; MCP pydantic models MIRROR them and are pinned by the cross-interface test (the drift guard). Document in docs (new docs/output-schemas.md or architecture.md section) + link from README/CLAUDE/event-schema.md.
* Test pattern + registry home CONFIRMED reusable.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: DONE (commit pending). Coverage guard test_schema_coverage.py: every schema wired in OUTPUT_SCHEMAS, every entry resolves, every --output-advertising command (17, discovered from dispatcher source) is covered. Fixed ready->ticket_state registry gap. docs/output-schemas.md (flag + per-command contract + source-of-truth decision + drift guard) linked from README/CLAUDE/event-schema/architecture. Full suite 1300 passed; probe 141.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
